answer:
**'Gulshan' (literally "rose garden") is a recurrent metaphorical setting in the Urdu ghazals and nazms of the Falsafa corpus, particularly in works by Mirza Ghalib and Allama Iqbal.**[^1][^2]

It draws from Persian poetic conventions (inherited in Urdu *shayari*), evoking a lush paradise of blooming roses (*gul*), nightingales (*bulbul*), and springtime renewal—often as a locus for romantic longing, divine beauty, transience, or cosmic disorder. The garden delights in the beloved's presence,[^3] questions the rose's honor outside it,[^4] or descends into chaos amid sorrow.[^5] In Iqbal, it hosts revelers indifferent to spiritual anthems of freedom.[^6]

This motif underscores themes of love's ecstasy/pain (*ishq*), fleeting worldly splendor (*fana*), and the soul's quest amid beauty's illusions—no explicit treatise defines it, but its usage permeates these verses as a archetypal backdrop.

[^1]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 56 ("Gulshan ko teri sohbat..."), [translation](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/56-gulshan-ko-teri-sohbat-az-bas-keh-khush-aayi-hai/translation/).
[^2]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 20 ("Shikwa"), [translation](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/translation/).
[^3]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 56: "The garden by your presence is so exquisitely pleased, / that every bud's unfolding is an open-armed release." [/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/56-gulshan-ko-teri-sohbat-az-bas-keh-khush-aayi-hai/translation/#p-ea6dfe]
[^4]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 2: "What grace has any rose if its garden is not within?" [/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/02-aabroo-kya-khaak-uss-gul-ki-ke-gulshan-mein-nahin/translation/#p-34aa92]
[^5]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 57: "The garden's order follows a different law today..." [/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/57-gulshan-mein-bandobast-ba-rang-e-digar-hai-aaj/translation/#p-90109e]
[^6]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 20 (transliteration): "Baada-kash ghair hain gulshan mein lab-e-joo baithe" ("Wine-drinking strangers sit lip-to-jar in the garden"). [/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/20-shikwa/transliteration/#p-a56897]
